claim: g3-llmproxy bracket verdict rule: the human record deliberately left the two-annotator combination rule open, so the proxy PRE-DECLARES a two-sided bracket instead of consuming that decision — PASS-analog requires the UNION-rule necessity rate's Wilson 95% UB <= 0.10 (upper bracket), FAIL-analog requires the CONCORDANT-rule rate's Wilson 95% LB > 0.10 (lower bracket); each verdict direction must clear under the combination rule LEAST favourable to it; anything between is INCONCLUSIVE.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/g3-llmproxy.json; poc/g3-llmproxy/design.md S4
rationale: Any single combination rule would bias one verdict direction and would also pre-empt an open human-round decision; bracketing is mechanical, conservative in both directions, and leaves the human round's degrees of freedom untouched.

claim: g3-llmproxy estimand gap: HS3 is a claim about human ordinary usage; LLM pass-A truth-of-claim and pass-B literal condition-satisfaction judgments approximate human judgments with unproven systematic offsets (the literalism channel can push the necessity-violation rate up via hyper-literal condition failures or down via charitable reading); neither proxy verdict direction measures the human rates.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/g3-llmproxy.json extrapolation_envelope_verbatim clause 1
resolution_path: Run the frozen human g3 (two independent annotators) on the identical 200 instances; per-item human-vs-proxy deltas on q1/q2 measure the offset and its direction directly.

claim: g3-llmproxy escape + coverage gates: cannot-say is neither violation nor satisfaction (the pinned protocol's stated treatment); per judge per pass, cannot-say > 5% or no-label > 5% => INSTRUMENT-INVALID (the protocol's ~5% re-examination line made mechanical, fail-closed); an instance is decisive for a judge iff q1 and q2 are both in {yes,no}; n_dual_decisive >= 170 of 200 keeps both Wilson branches reachable (PASS-analog up to ~11 union violations, FAIL-analog from ~24 concordant, at the floor).
backing_ref: registry/experiments/g3-llmproxy.json; data/instance-descriptions/annotation-protocol.md (open run-stage decision 3)
rationale: The human protocol proposed but did not freeze the cannot-say treatment; the proxy must fix it mechanically pre-run, and adopting the protocol's own proposal at its own ~5% line is the minimal-choice instantiation.

claim: g3-llmproxy probe-B construction: 30 seed-pinned instances are presented in pass-B form with the condition set of a DIFFERENT concept (concept-level Sattolo cycle over the 20 condition_set_ids), original text + bindings verbatim; 'no' is the correct answer by construction (another concept's conditions, roles largely unbound); only q2=yes counts as false satisfaction (cannot-say is non-endorsement); false-satisfaction >= 0.30 or probe coverage < 27/30 invalidates that judge's instrument; probes never enter counts.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/g3-llmproxy.json; poc/g3-llmproxy/design.md S5
rationale: The direct pass-B analog of the house content-scramble control: it bounds the yes-saying channel that would fabricate a low violation rate, is constructible from pinned materials without gold labels, and unbound-role artifacts are handled by counting only affirmative endorsement as failure.

claim: m0a-llmproxy stand-in scope + judge choice: the M0a pre-registration names HUMAN annotation; the current provisional P/R (strict 0.82/0.8865) were produced by a NON-BLIND, project-contexted Claude-family agent and are stamped PROVISIONAL; the proxy record replaces them with a blind, kernel-instance-naive, CROSS-VENDOR judge (gpt-5.6-sol effort low via npx-pinned codex-cli 0.144.1) over the same 300 pinned items — upgrading exactly blindness and author-family independence, never species-of-judge; cross-vendor is load-bearing because a Claude-family judge would correlate with the comparator labels and with the Claude-family mapper/gloss/judging work-products.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/m0a-llmproxy.json; poc/m0a-llmproxy/design.md S1, S3
rationale: The agent-judged stopgap is the weakest link in the published M0a numbers (non-blind, author-adjacent); a blind cross-vendor proxy is a strict control upgrade available now, while the human pass remains the only discharger of the pre-registration — the f2b-transfer-llmproxy pattern applied to an annotation measurement.

claim: m0a-llmproxy rendering contract: per-stratum blind renderings built mechanically from pinned bytes only — concept/prime items show the passage + marked token + the mapped target's gloss WITHOUT any urn/id; abstain items show the recorded candidates as a numbered gloss list; none items show the full pinned kernel-v0 surface inventory (54 concept lemmas + 65 prime exponents, one-line senses, byte-identical across items); the stated M0a judging criteria are carried on the sheets verbatim; the judge never sees urns, stratum labels, the words kernel/NSM/mapper, or that a mapping system produced the proposed sense; per-call blinding grep audit, any hit aborts.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/m0a-llmproxy.json; poc/m0a-llmproxy/design.md S2
rationale: The human instructions embedded in annotation-sample.jsonl presuppose knowledge of what the targets denote; a blind judge must receive that denotation as gloss bytes, and gloss-rendering-without-ids is the minimal disclosure that makes the task judgeable while keeping the judge kernel-instance-naive.

claim: m0a-llmproxy estimator reuse: precision/recall formulas, stratum populations (concept 117563 / prime 504500 / abstain 181388 / none 2958417), the strict/lenient duality, and the none-stratum Clopper-Pearson recall lower bound are reused EXACTLY from the published mapper/m0 compute-pr.py + m0a-report.json, with denominators restricted to labelled items; the none-stratum thinness (50 items over 2.96M tokens = 78% of token mass) carries over UNCHANGED — the proxy fixes nothing about the sampling design.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/m0a-llmproxy.json pins.artifact_hashes; mapper/m0/compute-pr.py
rationale: Estimator identity is what makes the proxy numbers a drop-in replacement for the agent-judged provisionals and makes the eventual human pass a per-item comparable readout; changing the estimator would silently change the estimand.

claim: m0a-llmproxy agent-delta reading: per-stratum raw agreement between the blind proxy labels and the 2026-07-07 non-blind agent labels measures blindness effects and judge-family effects JOINTLY; within this design the two are unattributable, so the delta is reported-only and licenses no statement about which judge is closer to truth.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/m0a-llmproxy.json endpoint sec-agent-delta
resolution_path: The human pass over the same 300 items: human-vs-proxy and human-vs-agent per-item agreement attribute the joint delta into judge-error components directly.

claim: m0a-llmproxy probe design: the deranged-gloss probe (20 concept + 20 prime items, per-stratum Sattolo cycle over distinct sampled targets, seed m0alp/1|probe|20260710) presents another target's gloss on the same token/context, making 'incorrect' the correct answer by construction (near-synonym collision risk disclosed); false-endorsement >= 0.30 or probe coverage < 36/40 invalidates the instrument; probes never enter labels or P/R.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/m0a-llmproxy.json; poc/m0a-llmproxy/design.md S4
rationale: Direct transplant of the f2b-transfer-llmproxy deranged-gloss control to the sense-judgment task: it bounds the gloss-endorsement leniency channel that would fabricate high proxy precision, at ~13% of the run's calls.

claim: llmproxy stand-in policy (shared, binding on g9-llmproxy, g3-llmproxy, m0a-llmproxy): per maintainer request (2026-07-10), blind kernel-instance-naive LLM judges stand in for the outstanding human annotation/review passes so the experiments run now and are human-verified later, exactly on the f2b-transfer-llmproxy model. Every proxy record: (a) takes a FRESH id and leaves the human record/pre-registration byte-untouched as the sole gold-standard adjudicator; (b) envelopes its verdict as a WEAK FEASIBILITY PROXY that substitutes for no part of the human pass, is quarantined and uncitable-as-final, and licenses ONLY programme sequencing / continued investment, never a claim upgrade; (c) never fills the human record's pins, never consumes its annotation round, and never triggers the human record's DAG consequences.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/f2b-transfer-llmproxy.json (frozen precedent) + registry/experiments/g9-llmproxy.json, g3-llmproxy.json, m0a-llmproxy.json
rationale: The f2b-transfer-llmproxy precedent (frozen, run, PASS, audit-confirmed, Fable-assessed) established that a fully-enveloped LLM stand-in yields real sequencing value (cheap-kill exposure, comparator pinning, operational de-risking) at near-zero cost without contaminating the human gold standard; this ASM fixes that discipline as the shared policy for the three new proxies. It falls away experiment-by-experiment as each human pass runs.

claim: llmproxy judge-mechanism catalogue (shared): the two lawful blind-judge mechanisms are (1) gpt-5.6-sol, reasoning effort low, via npx-pinned codex-cli 0.144.1 per the pinned judge-1p/truthstyle section-4.1 invocation form (empty out-of-repo workdir, user config ignored, memories + web search disabled, read-only sandbox, server-side output schema, zero-tool tripwire), and (2) claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 via headless claude -p per the truthstyle section-4.3 form (format-only pinned system prompt, tools disabled, no setting sources, no session persistence, thinking disabled, subscription auth asserted). Uniform contract: stateless per-item processes, first-valid-answer-final, <= 3 content attempts then no-label (never mapped to an escape), per-call blinding grep audit, preflight calibration first-attempt pass. The in-session Agent-tool sub-agent form is FORBIDDEN for blind judging (it injects project CLAUDE.md + auto-memory naming the project and NSM).
backing_ref: poc/truthstyle-2x2/judges-invocation.md (invocation semantics MEASURED at its 2026-07-10 staging); registry/experiments/g9-llmproxy.json, g3-llmproxy.json, m0a-llmproxy.json
rationale: Both mechanisms are already staged, measured, and hardened in this repo; reusing them byte-derived avoids new instrument risk and keeps every proxy's blinding auditable by the same mechanical checks.

claim: llmproxy tradition-familiarity direction (shared; generalises ASM-0021 beyond the f2b line): NSM literature, NSM-style explications and reductive-paraphrase conventions are plausibly in BOTH judge vendors' training data, so proxy judges may endorse familiar STYLE rather than judge content; this plausibly inflates endorsement-type statistics (g9 composite, m0a 'correct' rates) and has unproven direction on g3's violation rates; the deranged probes bound only the crudest form in each record.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/g9-llmproxy.json, g3-llmproxy.json, m0a-llmproxy.json extrapolation envelopes; ASM-0021
resolution_path: Each human pass on identical items (g9.review sheets, g3 instances, m0a annotation sample) measures the human-vs-proxy delta directly; jointly with the f2b human round these give the familiarity channel's sign and size across four task types.

claim: llmproxy comparator + non-contamination discipline (shared): every proxy pins its per-item labels so the eventual human pass yields a free per-item human-vs-proxy bias measurement; conversely, proxy outputs (labels, run logs, derived rates, verdicts) must NEVER be shown to a future human annotator/reviewer for the same items before their sheets are submitted, and proxy runs write NOTHING under the pinned human-materials trees (data/authored-explication-set/, data/instance-descriptions/, mapper/m0/ inputs); a violation contaminates the human gold standard and is a boundary-stop incident, escalate to the maintainer.
backing_ref: registry/experiments/g9-llmproxy.json, g3-llmproxy.json, m0a-llmproxy.json (stage_discipline + BINDING-NOTE assumptions)
rationale: The comparator role is the proxies' most durable scientific value (the f2b assessment's finding), and it survives only if the human passes stay naive; making the quarantine an explicit registered obligation binds every future runner and coordinator, not just this design.

claim: llmproxy escape + no-label accounting policy (shared): judge escape tokens (cannot-say / unclear / none-of-these where an endorsement is being tested) count as NON-ENDORSEMENT — against the PASS-side quantity, never silently dropped (direction disclosed per record); no-label after 3 invalid content attempts is an instrument event that leaves denominators and is capped by each record's coverage gate (~5%); escapes are never converted to labels and labels are never re-sampled (first-valid-answer-final).
backing_ref: registry/experiments/g9-llmproxy.json, g3-llmproxy.json, m0a-llmproxy.json instrument gates; data/d-adj-t-llmproxy/judge-1p-invocation.md (the f2b no-label contract)
rationale: Uniform, fail-closed accounting is what makes the three proxies' statistics comparable to each other and to their human passes; the f2b contract is adopted verbatim so no proxy can gain a PASS from escape-handling degrees of freedom.

claim: truthstyle-2x2 section-7.3 blinding-audit matching rule, AMENDMENT A1 (extends ASM-0360; ratified pre-data after the second ERR_DTS_BLINDING boundary stop): on the two HARNESS-CAPTURED surfaces events.jsonl and stderr.log - and NEVER on user-prompt.txt - a case-insensitive token match is additionally excluded from being a hit iff, in the ORIGINAL (pre-lowercasing) bytes, it lies wholly inside the base62 TAIL of a maximal Anthropic-format vendor identifier literal: a byte run matching (req|msg)_[0-9A-Za-z]{20,48} whose 4-byte prefix is lowercase-exact, whose adjacent bytes on both sides (if any) are outside [0-9A-Za-z_] (maximality; a run longer than 48 fails this check and is NOT excluded - fail-closed), with the token wholly within the {20,48} tail. The rule applies uniformly to all four pinned tokens; every byte outside qualifying tails is scanned byte-identically to ASM-0360; abort-on-hit semantics, the token list, the scanned surfaces, and all other tripwires are unchanged. This supersedes IN PART the ASM-0360 side-claim that matching for the non-hex tokens is byte-identical to the original scan.
backing_ref: poc/truthstyle-2x2/blinding-audit-clarification.md section 9 (amendment A1: measured abort facts, base62 rate analysis, leak-safety argument, normative code, residual-risk census); registry/corrections/truthstyle-2x2/4-blinding-audit-amendment-a1.json; poc/truthstyle-2x2/opus-runs/20260710T151231Z/judge-p3-haiku45/items/pos-102/c1_t0/events.jsonl (retained: token nsm at offset 1959 inside request_id req_011Cctc1sK9P4SujinnsMiWU; sha256 1b8d624be5118804d7027d0b58086e6aa6328a43afeb28ca827e1a0394dd72b7)
rationale: ASM-0360 excluded coincidences only for all-hex tokens inside hex runs, on the premise that non-hex blind tokens appear only via real leakage; that premise is contradicted by Anthropic base62 identifiers (req_/msg_ + 24 mixed-case alphanumerics, two per captured p3 attempt), where case-insensitive nsm occurs with p about 7.4e-4 per id, i.e. expected about 1.2 coincidental hits per perfectly blind 808-item p3 pass (abort probability about 0.7 under the null; observed: judge-p3 died at pos-102 on exactly this shape while the item bytes and answer were clean and p1/p2 judged the same item without incident). f2b inside base62 tails with non-hex neighbours was a second latent channel closed by the same rule. The exclusion is anchored on value FORMAT plus maximality, not on JSON field names: it parses nothing, works on non-JSON stderr, never excludes a whole field or line, and never applies to user-prompt.txt, so item contamination and judge echo (which lands in text blocks, not id tails) still fire. Rejected alternatives: dropping id fields from the scan surface (larger blind spot, needs JSON parsing); scrubbing ids from provenance pre-sweep (destroys retained-bytes re-derivability); changing the pinned section-4.3 p3 capture form (inverts the control hierarchy for a detector-side defect).
resolution_path: Mechanically re-derivable: the amended blinding-sweep.py derives vendor-id spans from the SAME _vendor_id_tail_spans helper the abort predicate uses and lists every exclusion with kind (hex_run with surrounding hex run / vendor_id with the full id literal), so the auditor can verify each exclusion is a machine identifier by inspection and can independently re-run the strict, ASM-0360, and A1 scans over the retained bytes of both run dirs. Revisit condition: any clarified-scan hit, any exclusion whose literal is not a machine identifier, or any vendor id format outside the (req|msg)_{20,48} shape coincidentally spelling a token, is a boundary stop back to Fable.

claim: truthstyle-2x2 re-run discipline for the pos-102 A1 false-positive abort: judge-p1 and judge-p2 passes of run poc/truthstyle-2x2/opus-runs/20260710T151231Z STAND as complete (808/808 each) and blinding-clean; ONLY judge-p3 re-runs, as a fresh full protocol (section-8 preflight then section-3-ordered main pass over all 808 items in the frozen p3 seed order) in a NEW run dir under the A1 executor, whose sha256 (with blinding-sweep.py's) is recorded in the new run dir before launch. The aborted p3 partial (pos-001..102) is VOID for labels and retained byte-verbatim as provenance; pos-102's captured bytes are NOT scrubbed and NOT re-captured in place (under A1 they are a listed vendor_id exclusion and the sweep is GREEN over the retained bytes). After MAIN_DONE p3 the coordinator copies the byte-identical p1/p2 run-summary and preflight-status files into the new run dir (shas recorded) and finalizes there; the A1 sweep must be GREEN over BOTH run dirs before the d-ts-labels ops amendment is written. Frozen pins, seeds, judge order, prompts, and the pinned section-4.3 capture form are byte-unchanged.
backing_ref: poc/truthstyle-2x2/blinding-audit-clarification.md sections 9.1 and 9.7; poc/truthstyle-2x2/p3-rerun-plan.md; registry/corrections/truthstyle-2x2/4-blinding-audit-amendment-a1.json; data/d-ts-labels/ listing 2026-07-10 (only p1/p2 responses exist; no p3 label was ever assembled, written, or read)
rationale: ASM-0361 (void the whole campaign) is expressly one-shot and its premises do not transfer: here two passes completed cleanly under the pinned protocol with responses mechanically written at pass end, the p3 abort is a detector-side false positive independent of their content, and the A1 predicate change alters no judge-visible byte, so label distributions cannot depend on it - the final gate is uniform because the A1 sweep re-applies the same predicate over the retained p1/p2 bytes. Resuming p3 at pos-103 is refused for the ASM-0361 reason (the spec defines no resume mechanism; the void partial's labels were never persisted, so resume would need new unpinned logic to re-ingest captured events). Disclosed: during diagnosis the coordinator read the void pos-102 capture (item steamfitter, raw answer bytes no); that sample is void, enters no statistic, the item is re-judged fresh, and first-valid-answer-is-final (ASM-0241) bars outcome-conditioned re-rolls; the re-run criterion is structural, not outcome-dependent. Budget: about 102 discarded p3 calls at the measured 0.0002-0.003 USD/call, inside the frozen usd_cap 40.
resolution_path: One-shot: applies only to the judge-p3 abort of run 20260710T151231Z at pos-102. Any future mid-pass abort is its own boundary stop; this entry is not a standing resume-or-restart policy. Discharged when the new p3 run dir carries the recorded executor shas, run-summary-p3.json, and GREEN blinding-sweep.json outputs for both run dirs.

claim: A-F0 scope and identity (reserved-block 0600-0619 use: A-F0 cached-prefix mint economics; the pre-cleared fork sub-test of N-IOC section 2.3 under the ASM-0463 ladder): A-F0 measures the MINT side of the coverage-growth track ONLY — the price vector ($/concept, $/legal record, tokens by cache class, wall latency) and the cached-prefix amortization curve of minting concepts into the modelAuthored tier via a claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 definer called DIRECTLY over the Messages API with prompt caching. This transport is a NEW pipeline identity per the s1 convention (the pinned call configuration is part of pipeline identity); the s1-G claude-CLI pipeline remains a distinct, unmodified baseline. Binding honesty caps: A-F0 is NEVER quotable as end-to-end viability of idea A or of any compression mechanism; net-positive mint economics is NECESSARY-NOT-SUFFICIENT — the consumption channel (K-A3 tokenizer-vocabulary-extension null, trained-compressor null, 1-token delivery, accuracy non-inferiority) stays entirely UNMEASURED and K-A4 mappability stays UNPRICED (ASM-0560); per ASM-0463 the result serves the WHOLE coverage-growth track (idea B's re-entry lever, the linter staircase), not idea A alone, and nothing in A-F0 may displace the feasibility-synthesis section-5 critical path.
backing_ref: docs/next/io-compression-signoff.md (ASM-0460, ASM-0463); docs/next/io-compression-ideas.md sections 2.2-2.3; registry/assessments/a-e2-census.json (proceed-to-A-F0 fork resolution, ASM-0563); registry/experiments/a-f0-mint-economics.json
rationale: Fixing scope and quotability at freeze closes the two known overclaim channels: reading a cheap mint as compression viability (consumption unmeasured), and reading the fork winner as an idea-A verdict (the mechanism serves the whole coverage-growth track).

claim: A-F0 price basis (frozen conversion table; every USD DV is API usage fields x this table): claude-haiku-4-5 input $1.00/MTok, output $5.00/MTok (thinking tokens bill as output); prompt-cache read $0.10/MTok (0.1x input); prompt-cache write $1.25/MTok at the 5-minute TTL (1.25x; the 1-hour TTL at $2.00/MTok is not used); Message Batches 50% discount on all token usage — used ONLY as a derived projection multiplier in A-F0, never measured; minimum cacheable prefix on Haiku 4.5 = 4096 tokens (shorter prefixes silently do not cache); caching is a strict prefix match over tools -> system -> messages and a cache entry becomes readable only after the first response begins streaming; thinking on Haiku 4.5 is the budgeted configuration only (type=enabled, budget_tokens >= 1024 and < max_tokens; the effort parameter is unsupported on Haiku 4.5; max_tokens=0 pre-warm requests are rejected when thinking is enabled). Source note: checked 2026-07-10 against the current Claude API reference bundle available to the design harness (models/pricing table cached 2026-06-24 plus the prompt-caching reference), byte-consistent with the N-IOC section-2.2 mechanics verified against live API docs 2026-07-09. The runner MUST re-verify this table at staging preflight against the live pricing/caching docs; any discrepancy is an ops amendment BEFORE first spend; raw usage fields are always logged so every dollar figure can be reconverted under a corrected table.
backing_ref: docs/next/io-compression-ideas.md section 2.2 (caching mechanics verified 2026-07-09); registry/experiments/a-f0-mint-economics.json; analysis/a_f0_mint_economics.py (embedded table)
rationale: The DVs need a deterministic usage->USD conversion fixed before any spend, because provider billing is not observable per call; pinning the table with a mandatory preflight re-verification makes every dollar figure reproducible from logged usage fields.

claim: A-F0 decision rule and margins (frozen): baseline = s1-G $0.078 API-equivalent per legal record (MEASURED, s1 report). An arm is LAWFUL iff it processed >= 55/60 concepts AND produced >= 5 legal records. PASS (mint economics SUSTAINED) iff the best lawful arm's measured usd_per_legal_record <= 0.75 x 0.078 = $0.05850 (at least a 25% improvement margin). FAIL (K-A1-mint fires: the cached-prefix definer is dead as an economics play) iff at least one lawful arm exists AND the minimum lawful usd_per_legal_record >= $0.078. Otherwise INCONCLUSIVE (improvement below margin, or no lawful arm). The 25% margin buys out the pipeline-switch cost of carrying a second pipeline identity plus the named baseline-comparability weaknesses (ASM-0607); the FAIL side deliberately requires NO improvement at all, so the kill cannot fire on margin noise.
backing_ref: data/haiku-tier/s1-experiments/s1-report.md sha256 238db0ddfaca614f30886afebfee219430062ed067c9f7d2ded83a489118e5b5 (G row: $0.0202/concept, 13/50 legal, ~$0.078/legal record); registry/experiments/a-f0-mint-economics.json
rationale: usd_per_legal_record self-penalizes low-yield arms; the lawfulness floor stops a 1-2-record fluke from winning best-arm; the asymmetric PASS-margin / no-improvement-FAIL split keeps both branches decidable at n=60 with ~15 expected legal records per arm under the s1-measured 26% yield.

claim: A-F0 arms and fork split (frozen): the four N-IOC section-2.3 arms, all framework-G gate-in-the-loop over the cached-prefix Messages-API transport — (a) current G output form (JSON record + minimal frame), thinking off; (b) direct-symbolic: record only, compact serialization, zero prose, thinking off; (c) = b with thinking enabled budget_tokens=1024; (d) = a with the same thinking budget. All arms run the A-F2(b) SOURCE-FED configuration (target word + pinned fetched extracts in the variable suffix), the current pipeline's provenance-keeping form: it is the conservative/pessimistic configuration for cached-prefix economics (maximal uncached suffix) and matches the s1-G baseline's input shape for a like-for-like $/legal-record comparison. The A-F2(a) word-only arm and the Batch-vs-interactive delta are DEFERRED to A-E1 (N-IOC assigns A-F2 to A-F0/A-E1 jointly; A-F0 takes the output-form x thinking fork). Time-to-first-token is dropped from the section-2.3 DV list (batch minting is throughput-bound, not TTFT-bound); wall latency per call is kept. Repair policy pinned to the volume-runner form: at most ONE gate-error-fed repair call, only for gate-failing attempted drafts. Sampling parameters omitted (API defaults), one stochastic sample per concept per arm as in s1; no determinism claim.
backing_ref: docs/next/io-compression-ideas.md sections 2.2-2.3 (arms, fork A-F2, worked estimate); registry/experiments/a-f0-mint-economics.json
rationale: Keeps A-F0 the cheapest decisive instrument: 4 cells x 60 concepts x <= 2 calls fits the ~$5-10 clearance; the deferred forks either strictly lower cost (word-only suffix, Batch discount) or need pilot scale (A-E1), so deferring them cannot flip an A-F0 kill.

claim: A-F0 sample (frozen): n=60 English concepts, identical across all four arms — 45 seed-pinned stratified draws from data/haiku-tier/inventory/inventory.jsonl (15 per band A/B/C; deterministic derivation pinned in the staged sample manifest; seed string 'a-f0/1|sample|20260710'), EXCLUDING lemmas already minted (data/haiku-tier/minted-urns.jsonl) and REQUIRING source-fetch success at staging (a fetch failure replaces the item with the next seed-order candidate in the same band; every replacement logged); plus the 15 s1 hand-authored-overlap anchors verbatim (begin, afraid, believe, break, find, death, conversation; dog, water, red, eat, sleep, house, money, music). English-only: the English framework-G pipeline (closed grounding lexicon, gates.mjs validators, pinned ref catalog) is the ONLY executable fail-closed mint path today; inventing a non-English definer inside a cost experiment would be new instrument risk with unpinnable provenance. Cross-lingual mint cost is NOT measured by A-F0 (ASM-0606 carries the expectation; A-E1 owns the measurement).
backing_ref: data/haiku-tier/inventory/inventory.jsonl sha256 61593d085b77f2d3d5f6a3b30ebe176a11afbcfdafcb41035fb06276b29769c7; data/haiku-tier/minted-urns.jsonl sha256 5f8b381ae9105096b8757da5bcde2080fb6f351852aeb96fefd1cc7b0dbbfe0a; data/haiku-tier/s1-experiments/s1-report.md sha256 238db0ddfaca614f30886afebfee219430062ed067c9f7d2ded83a489118e5b5
rationale: Stratified bands plus the s1 anchor set keep definer difficulty comparable to the measured baseline and give the fidelity spot-check its anchors; excluding already-minted lemmas avoids self-reference collisions against the accumulated catalog carried in the cached prefix.

claim: A-F0 amortization protocol + cache-integrity instrument gate (frozen): concurrency 1; fixed arm order a -> d -> b -> c; two prefix groups by byte identity ({a,d} and {b,c} — the output-form instruction differs between groups so they cache separately; the thinking toggle does NOT invalidate the tools/system cache tier per the documented invalidation hierarchy, so arms d and c run against a warm cache); the first call of each prefix group is the cache-writing call (no pre-warm call is needed at concurrency 1, and max_tokens=0 pre-warms are rejected when thinking is enabled). Cold-start amortization curves are defined on arms a and b ONLY (cumulative mean usd/concept at k in {1,5,10,20,40,60}); steady-state marginal usd/concept (mean over each arm's last 20 concepts) is defined on all four arms; the warm start of d/c is a disclosed consequence of the fixed order, not a distortion of the steady-state estimand. TTL economics: the 5-minute cache is expected to stay warm at the s1-measured ~4-8 s/call cadence; any idle gap > TTL surfaces as a logged cache_creation event whose cost stays IN the measured totals. INSTRUMENT GATE cache_integrity: cache_read_input_tokens > 0 on >= 90% of calls after each prefix group's first call, AND both serialized prefixes measure >= 4096 tokens via count_tokens at staging — otherwise the run measured the WRONG MECHANISM (uncached economics) and the verdict is INSTRUMENT-INVALID, never FAIL/PASS.

claim: A-F0 keyless-channel adjudication (designer ruling on the maintainer's run-mechanism question, 2026-07-10; binds execution; amends NOTHING in the frozen record): the headless claude-CLI subscription channel (claude -p --model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 --output-format stream-json, fresh --no-session-persistence process per call) is INVALID as an A-F0 transport, and no ops amendment can port A-F0 onto it. Grounds: (1) the pinned call configuration IS the mechanism under test and is not instantiable through the CLI wrapper — the CLI owns request serialization, tool-block composition, dynamic system-prompt sections (per-machine cwd/env/git-status content sits in the system prompt by default; the exclude flag only relocates it into the first user message), and cache_control breakpoint placement, none observable or byte-pinnable from outside, and it exposes no per-call control of thinking budget_tokens (arms c/d) or max_tokens (the --effort flag is not budgeted thinking and effort is unsupported on Haiku 4.5 per ASM-0601); (2) the amortization endpoint (sec-amortization, H-AF0-AMORT) is unidentifiable on that channel — usage cache_creation/cache_read fields aggregate the whole request, so definitional-prefix reuse cannot be separated from the CLI's own wrapper-prefix caching; (3) the cache_integrity instrument gate goes vacuous there (wrapper-prefix cache reads alone can satisfy the >= 90% cache-read-positive floor while the definitional prefix re-bills uncached on every call — the exact wrong-mechanism failure the gate exists to catch), and the prefix_min_cacheable gate requires /v1/messages/count_tokens, which the keyless channel does not expose; (4) the subscription channel yields API-EQUIVALENT projections, not API-billed dollars — pricing its usage fields by the ASM-0601 table is the s1 accounting basis, not a Batch-style derived multiplier on the mechanism's own measured usage, and it erases A-F0's registered purpose of making the cost side of the ledger real; (5) running through the CLI reverts the pipeline identity under test (ASM-0600, no-CLI-fallback clause) and collapses the registered treatment — transport + serialization differences ARE the treatment per ASM-0607 — into a CLI-variant-vs-CLI-variant comparison the frozen ASM-0602 thresholds were never sized for. Consequences: the S7 API-key hard precondition STANDS; absent key remains a BOUNDARY STOP to the maintainer; any keyless CLI-transport fork study (a-vs-b output form only; thinking arms not instantiable) would be a NEW experiment record with its own thresholds and envelope, never an A-F0 amendment, and nothing in this ruling creates one.
backing_ref: docs/next/a-f0-keyless-channel-adjudication.md; registry/experiments/a-f0-mint-economics.json (frozen_sha256 fb4147ccfa6f88936effa991616927317f6aaed98eb24b38e11984b334bf5dfc); docs/next/a-f0-mint-economics-spec.md S2/S5/S7; ASM-0600; ASM-0601; ASM-0605; ASM-0607
rationale: The freeze names the direct Messages API as the pipeline identity under test and forbids CLI fallback precisely because the CLI silently reverts that identity; the keyless channel additionally blinds both cache-side instruments and the billing basis, so an amended A-F0 on that channel would quote costs of a mechanism it never ran. Registering the refusal makes the boundary stop durable against re-litigation and keeps the frozen thresholds attached to the mechanism they price.

claim: llmproxy Gate-A audit-applicability ruling (Fable interpretive ruling, 2026-07-10; binding on g9-llmproxy and m0a-llmproxy, and the default for future llmproxy stand-ins unless a design supersedes it): the house Gate-A discipline — the audit gate fires on PASS only; FAIL/INCONCLUSIVE verdicts stand MEASURED-UNAUDITED with audit.state N/A — applies to QUARANTINED weak-proxy records UNCHANGED. The ASM-0553 quarantine caps the MEANING of a proxy verdict, not its arithmetic: a proxy PASS whose numbers become the programme's provisional citables (m0a-llmproxy P/R replace the agent-judged provisionals in every artifact) requires the cross-vendor Gate-A mechanical recompute exactly as a primary PASS does, and the pending human-gold pass CANNOT discharge that function — it measures a different estimand on a different timeline and never re-verifies the estimator arithmetic. Therefore: m0a-llmproxy REQUIRES a Codex Gate-A audit before its PASS confirms (auditor family overlap with the GPT-family judge disclosed in the frozen stage_discipline; acceptable because Gate-A is byte recomputation, never judge validation); g9-llmproxy (FAIL-analog) is audit-NOT-applicable and its verdict object's audit.state N/A is correct as emitted.

rationale: The Gate-A audit exists to stop an erroneous self-favoring verdict from licensing investment or citation; a FAIL is self-disfavoring and its pre-registered consequence (pause + escalate at proxy scope) is conservative, so auditing it adds nothing the chain/lint rails do not already give — the g8 precedent states 'the Gate-A PASS-upgrade is inapplicable to a FAIL'. A PASS whose P/R become the programme's provisional numbers is exactly the case the mechanical recompute protects: population weighting, strict/lenient duality and the Clopper-Pearson bound are the error class an independent recompute catches. Falls away per-record when each audit lands or each human pass supersedes the proxy.
